title: Caloncoba aristata
taxonomy: Caloncoba aristata belongs to the family Guttiferae, a group of flowering plants. However, the family Guttiferae is now considered an outdated name and has been replaced by the family Clusiaceae in modern classifications. Caloncoba aristata is native to tropical Africa and is a member of the genus Caloncoba, which is a small genus of flowering plants. Caloncoba aristata is further classified as a species of flowering plant within the kingdom Plantae. The complete classification of Caloncoba aristata is therefore Caloncoba aristata, Clusiaceae, Caloncoba, and Plantae.

Caloncoba aristata is traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro: Caloncoba aristata, also known as the African Caloncoba, is a tropical tree species native to the forests of West and Central Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Caloncoba aristata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Caloncoba aristata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
